code_items.intent: Introduced an ActiveCache class that extends Cache with EventEmitter functionality, enabling event-driven operations by emitting "set" and "delete" events when cache entries are modified.
Introduced an ActiveCache class that extends Cache with EventEmitter functionality, enabling event-driven operations by emitting "set" and "delete" events when cache entries are modified.
Introduced an ActiveCache class that extends Cache with EventEmitter functionality, enabling event-driven operations by emitting "set" and "delete" events when cache entries are modified.
Introduced an ActiveCache class that extends Cache with EventEmitter functionality, enabling event-driven operations by emitting "set" and "delete" events when cache entries are modified.
Introduced a new `Cache` class extending `Collection` with a `default` method that conditionally sets a value only if the key doesn't already exist, returning whether a new entry was inserted.
Introduced a new `Cache` class extending `Collection` with a `default` method that conditionally sets a value only if the key doesn't already exist, returning whether a new entry was inserted.
Introduced a new `Cache` class extending `Collection` with a `default` method that conditionally sets a value only if the key doesn't already exist, returning whether a new entry was inserted.
Added new Cache and ActiveCache extension modules to the library exports, introducing caching functionality alongside existing base and manager components.

Refactored the Collection class to improve code readability and consistency by renaming parameters and variables to more descriptive names, standardizing iteration variable names (keyContext, value), and deprecating `some`/`every` methods in favor of `someSatisfy`/`everySatisfy` with deprecation warnings. & Renamed the parameter `Key` to `keyContext` and `Value` to `value` in the standard collection methods (`set`, `delete`, `get`, `has`), and reorganized the code by adding section comments ("Standard methods" and "Migration methods") to improve clarity and distinguish between standard and migration-related functionality.

The changes refactor the DataStore constructor to delegate initialization to the parent Collection class via `super(iterable)`, removing manual iteration logic, and standardize parameter naming conventions (e.g., `Key` to `keyContext`, `Model` to `model`) for consistency.

Refactored the `BaseManager` class to adopt modern JavaScript conventions by renaming parameters to camelCase, replacing `Object.defineProperty` with direct property assignments, and improving the iteration logic for initializing the cache with a `for...in` loop that handles both arrays and objects.
